sentences.text: You are going to listen to five texts.
For each of them, answer the two questions given.
Mark the correct answer A, B, or C.
You have 20 seconds to look through the task.
You will hear the recording twice.
Text 1
David and I became friends when we were kids.
We went to different schools, but David and his family lived in the house next to us.
I don't exactly remember what we did.
I don't remember what made us so close.
We were so different.
I loved outdoor games.
He loved indoor games.
I enjoyed reading books.
He spent a lot of time in front of his computer.
In high school, my dad once took me fishing, and since then it has become an important part of my lifestyle.
At that same time, David started making models of airplanes.
He never showed any interest in my hobby.
But we liked to share our impressions,
and we would spend evenings together.
Unfortunately, David and his family moved to another town some time ago.
However, we are Facebook friends and we are still in close contact with each other.

Text 2

These majestic mountains, with their terrifying narrow and winding roads and hidden villages,
which you cannot reach in winter, are the source of many legends. According to one Greek legend,
the Caucasus Mountains were one of the columns holding up the world. And another legend says
that it was here that Zeus, the Greek god, ordered to tie up Prometheus with unbreakable chains.
Prometheus was tortured day and night by a giant eagle. Today, the Caucasus Mountains are
increasingly becoming a destination for climbers, hikers, or skiers looking for adventure.
What's the highest mountain range in Europe? The Alps? Wrong. It is the Caucasus Mountains,
marking the border between Georgia and Russia.
While the highest peak, Elbrus, is in Russia, Georgia is proud to have the second highest
mountain peak, Shkara, which is 5,193 meters high, nearly 400 meters higher than Mont Blanc,
the peak on the border of France and Italy. These majestic mountains, with their terrifying
narrow and winding roads and hidden villages, which you cannot reach in winter, are the source
of many legends. According to one Greek legend, the Caucasus Mountains are the highest mountain
ranges in the world. The Caucasus Mountains were one of the columns holding up the world.
And another legend says that it was here that Zeus, the Greek god, ordered to tie up Prometheus
with unbreakable chains. Prometheus was tortured day and night by a giant eagle. Today, the
Caucasus Mountains are increasingly becoming a destination for climbers, hikers, or skiers
looking for adventure.
Text 3.
Harbin International Ice and Snow Sculpture Festival is an annual winter festival that
takes place in Harbin, China. It is the largest ice and snow festival in the world. Today,
it has become an international festival and competition. At first, participants of the
festival were Chinese, but then other artists from Japan and Malaysia joined in. The festival
demonstrates the world's biggest ice sculptures, which are displayed throughout the city. The
festival is a festival of the world's largest ice sculptures, which are displayed throughout the city.
The festival starts on January 5th and lasts one month. However, if the weather stays cold,
the figures made of ice often open earlier and last longer. During the festival, there are night
tours in many parts of the city that are lit with ice lanterns. Winter activities during the
festival include alpine skiing, winter swimming in the river, and the ice lantern exhibition in
the city garden. The Harbin International Ice and Snow Sculpture Festival is an annual winter
festival that takes place in Harbin, China. It is the largest ice and snow festival in the world.
Today, it has become an international festival and competition. At first, participants of the
festival were Chinese, but then other artists from Japan and Malaysia joined in. The festival
demonstrates the world's biggest ice sculptures, which are displayed throughout the city.
The festival starts on January 5th and lasts one month. However, if the weather stays cold,
the figures made of ice often open earlier and last longer. During the festival, there are night
tours in many parts of the city that are lit with ice lanterns. Winter activities during the
festival include alpine skiing, winter swimming in the river, and the ice lantern exhibition in
the city garden. Text 4. A 25-year-old Roman general and statesman, Julius Caesar, was sailing
the Aegean Sea when he was kidnapped by his father. He was a man of great strength and was
a great man. While staying with the pirates, Julius Caesar spent most of his time composing
poetry. He would then read his writings to the pirates. Julius Caesar demanded from the pirates
not to talk while he was sleeping or resting. The pirates started to respect him and allowed
him to move freely around their island. The pirates who kidnapped Caesar asked for silver
money to let him free. Caesar sent his men to his homeland to collect the silver for him.
While his men were collecting the required silver, Julius Caesar was alone with the pirates,
but instead of feeling fear, he treated them as if they were his servants. Caesar acted as a leader
rather than a prisoner. However, Caesar promised to punish them after he was freed. As soon as
Caesar was free again, he kept his promise and killed all the pirates and returned all of his
silver. A 25-year-old Roman general and statesman, Julius Caesar, was sailing the Aegean Sea when he
was kidnapped by Sicilian pirates. While staying with the pirates, Julius Caesar spent most of his
time composing poetry. He would then read his writings to the pirates. Julius Caesar demanded
from the pirates not to talk while he was sleeping or resting. The pirates started to
respect him and allowed him to move freely around their island. The pirates who kidnapped Caesar
asked for silver money to let him free. Caesar sent his men to his homeland to collect the
silver for him. While his men were collecting the required silver, Julius Caesar was alone with
the pirates, but instead of feeling fear, he treated them as if they were his servants.
Caesar acted as a leader rather than a prisoner. However, Caesar promised to punish them after he
was freed. As soon as Caesar was free again, he kept his promise and killed all the pirates and
returned all of his silver. Text 5. Georgia remembers a lot of
historical battles which make Georgians proud today. One of them was the Battle of Dugori between
the Kingdom of Georgia and the Seljuk Empire at the beginning of the 12th century. Georgia's
victory at Dugori marked the beginning of the medieval Georgian Golden Age and is known as a
miraculous victory. On the morning of 12 August 1121, 200 Georgian soldiers left the camp and
rode to the enemy's side. They pretended that they wanted to run away, but they were wrong. They
were not able to do so. The Seljuk army was disorganized and the Georgians won the battle.
Nowadays, Georgians continue to remember the Battle of August 12th of 1121 and celebrate the
annual September festival known as Ditgoroba. The preparations for the festival start as early as
May. The Georgians were not able to return to their home country, so they were forced to leave the
country. The battle of Dugori is one of the most notable battles of Georgian history.
Georgia remembers a lot of historical battles which make Georgians proud today. One of them
was the Battle of Dugori between the Kingdom of Georgia and the Seljuk Empire at the beginning of
the 12th century. Georgia's victory at Dugori marked the beginning of the medieval Georgian
Golden Age and is known as a miraculous victory. On the morning of 12 August 1121, 200 Georgian
soldiers left the camp and rode to the enemy's side. They pretended that they wanted to run away
from the Georgian camp. The Seljuk commanders welcomed the Georgian soldiers. As soon as the
Georgians entered the enemy's camp, they attacked and killed most of the enemies.
The Seljuk army became disorganized and the Georgians won the battle.
Nowadays, Georgians continue to remember the Battle of August 12th of 1121 and celebrate the
annual September festival known as Didguroba. The preparations for the festival start as early
as in May. The Battle of Dugori is one of the most notable battles of Georgian history.
Task 2. You are going to listen to one text with eight questions. Mark the correct answer
A, B, or C. You now have 30 seconds to look through the task. You will then hear the recording
twice.
Hello, my full name is Shakira Isabel Mebarak Ripol, but the whole world knows me as Shakira.
I come from Colombia. Today, I'm a successful singer, but there was a time when my career
almost came to an end. It happened in late 2017. I suffered from a problem in my throat,
and I could hardly speak. I always thought that at some point in my life,
some things would go away, like attractiveness, youth, all of that stuff.
But I never thought that my voice would lead me, because it's so fundamental for my personality.
So, it was extremely difficult for me when I couldn't sing. There were times I couldn't
even get out of bed. I was so depressed. There is a joke that a man often wishes to have married
a dumb wife, or a wife who cannot speak. But when I had to remain quiet, my partner, Gerard Piquet,
felt like one of those ex-prisoners who is given his freedom and doesn't know what to do.
I was very upset and pessimistic. I have never fought with my partner so much as in those times
when I was unable to speak. I had to communicate through signs, and nobody could understand me.
My sons, two and four then, couldn't read, so writing didn't help. When my voice eventually
returned, without an operation, I was extremely happy. I was glad that I had not agreed to surgery.
I did some schoolwork on the track in my teaching. I had tried meditation,
even going to Lourdes, a major religious site for Catholic believers to get holy water.
I don't know exactly what helped me, but I was ready for the stage. My sixth tour,
called the El Dorado Tour, had been postponed due to my throat problems. But when I finally
went on that tour, it was very different. I was out there because I wanted to feel the pleasure of singing.
Say higher! Joe Burwell, 50 years older than I was, notre belle dramatic human,ın
go to the stage and sing to convince myself that I'm good and that people like me. But the El Dorado
tour every night on stage was a gift. I think this tragic experience has made me a better singer.
I had to overcome so many difficulties to become an international pop singer.
Today, I live in Barcelona with Gerard and our two sons. When I first went on a tour after my
sons were born, I had no idea how this was going to work. At some point, I thought it was going to
be impossible. It is true that being separated from my sons was hard. My kids were so little,
I missed even their bad behavior. I tried to arrange the dates so that they would coincide
with school holidays and they could be with me, but other times they stayed at home with Gerard.
Being a mother is the hardest job I've ever done.
It could affect your creativity unless you protect your personal space.
When I'm with my children, I try to tell them about problems their dad and I had to overcome,
but I never overload them with every detail of my career or every victory. They weren't born when
I was back in Colombia and every single door was shut in my face. Those are the stories I want to
tell them because life isn't always easy. Not everything happens as you've planned. When my
children attend a concert, I try to tell them about the problems they're facing. I try to tell
them that they're not the only ones who are being treated badly in Colombia. I try to tell them
that if they don't attend my show for the first time, I saw that they looked a little confused.
I'm trying to give them a normal life, but it is not always possible.
There is huge social inequality in Colombia, so I initiated a foundation to help schools and
children in Colombia. After I became popular, I knew I had to succeed and make money because only
that way I could do something for my country. Hello, my full name is a girl named Mikaela. I'm
a woman. I can do anything I want. I'm a woman. I have a job. I'm a woman. I'm a woman. I'm a man. I'm a woman.
